title: GraphQueryAsJSON
description: Schema for LLM-generated graph queries that compile to ClickHouse SQL

nodes.description: Node selectors for the query. Single-node queries use a 1-element array.

aggregations.description: Aggregation functions to apply. Required when query_type is 'aggregation'.

group_by.description: Aggregation group keys. "node" returns a node object; "node.property" returns a scalar value.

path.description: Path finding configuration. Required when query_type is 'path_finding'.

neighbors.description: Neighbors configuration. Required when query_type is 'neighbors'.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

cursor.description: Keyset cursor. First page: omit 'after'. Later pages: pass the prior response's pagination.next_cursor; stop when it is absent. NULL sort keys paginate (they sort last).

page_size.description: Max rows per page; a page may return fewer after redaction.

after.description: Continuation token from pagination.next_cursor. Changing the query (filters, order_by, shape) invalidates it; restart pagination.

aggregation_sort.description: Aggregation row ordering as an output column name (aggregation alias or group-key alias): "column" (ascending) or "-column" (descending).

options.description: Consumer-level preferences that affect result presentation, not query semantics.

nodes.description: Neighbors queries take exactly 1 node: the center

direction.description: Aggregation only supports outgoing or incoming direction.

group_by.description: Required for disconnected multi-node aggregation; otherwise add relationships.

then.description: Only aggregation queries may group results.
Multi-node traversal requires nodes + relationships. Single-node traversal requires exactly 1 node and no relationships.

nodes.description: Multi-node traversal requires at least 2 nodes

relationships.description: Multi-node traversal requires at least 1 relationship

nodes.description: Path finding requires at least 2 nodes (start and end)

Identifier.description: Safe SQL identifier: starts with letter/underscore, alphanumeric/underscore only, max 64 chars

QueryType.description: Type of graph query to execute

EntityType.description: Valid entity types corresponding to GitLab data types (e.g., User, Project, MergeRequest). Validated against ontology at runtime.

NodeProperties.description: Property definitions per node label. Populated dynamically from ontology. Keys are entity names, values describe available properties.

additionalProperties.description: Properties available for a specific node label. Keys are property names, values are property schemas.

type.description: JSON Schema type of the property

items.description: For array types, describes the items

NodeSelector.description: Selector for matching nodes in the graph

id.description: Variable identifier for this node (e.g., 'i', 'mr', 'user'). Used to reference this node in relationships and aggregations.

entity.description: Entity type (e.g., User, Project, MergeRequest). Determines which table to query.

columns.description: Columns to return for this node. Use '*' for all columns, or specify an array of column names. If omitted, the node's default_columns from the ontology are returned. Mandatory columns (id, type) are always included for redaction.

filters.description: Property filters. Keys are property names; values are an equality value, an operator object, or an array of operator objects. Limited to prevent query complexity explosion.

node_ids.description: Specific node IDs to filter by. Accepts integers or digit strings.

id_property.description: Which property to use as ID (default: 'id')

ColumnSelection.description: Column selection for a node. Use '*' for all columns or an array of specific column names.

oneOf.description: Select all columns for this entity
List of specific column names to select

IdRange.description: Inclusive ID range filter. Endpoints accept integers or digit strings.

FilterEntry.description: Filter(s) on a single property. A bare value is an equality match; an operator object AND-combines its operator keys; an array of operator objects AND-combines entries (only needed to repeat an operator).

oneOf.description: AND-combined operator objects, for repeating an operator on the same property.

OperatorFilter.description: Operator-keyed filter; multiple keys AND-combine, e.g. {"gte": a, "lt": b}.

is_null.description: true matches NULL; false matches non-NULL.

is_not_null.description: true matches non-NULL; false matches NULL.

token_match.description: Exact word match. Only on fields that support word matching.

all_tokens.description: All words present. Only on fields that support word matching.

any_tokens.description: Any word present. Only on fields that support word matching.

FilterValue.description: A filter value - can be a number (integer or float), string, boolean, or list

SearchPattern.description: Substring or word-match pattern. Patterns shorter than 3 characters are rejected at compile time (traversal_path exempt).

RelationshipSelector.description: Selector for relationship traversal between nodes

from.description: Source node variable ID (must match a node's id field)

to.description: Target node variable ID (must match a node's id field)

max_hops.description: Maximum number of relationship hops. Limited to prevent resource exhaustion.

filters.description: Filters on relationship properties. Limited to prevent query complexity explosion.

RelationshipType.description: Relationship type specification - single, multiple, or wildcard

RelationshipTypeName.description: Valid relationship type names. Validated against ontology at runtime.

Direction.description: Direction to traverse relationships. 'outgoing' follows from_table → to_table, 'incoming' reverses it.

target.description: Node variable to aggregate over. If omitted, aggregates all matching nodes.

property.description: Property to aggregate. Required for sum, avg, min, max functions.

alias.description: Output column alias for the aggregation result

GroupByKey.description: Group key for aggregation rows. "node" groups by the node (entity object per bucket); "node.property" groups by the property (scalar per bucket). Object form adds an alias and/or date truncation.

truncate.description: Truncate a Date/DateTime property before grouping; requires a "node.property" key. minute/hour require node_ids or a filter on the property.

max_depth.description: Maximum path length (number of hops). SECURITY: Maximum 3 enforced.

rel_types.description: Relationship types to traverse. Required when an endpoint uses filters or id_range (not node_ids) to constrain the frontier. Optional when both endpoints use node_ids.

NeighborsConfig.description: Configuration for neighbors queries. The center is the query's single node.

direction.description: Direction to traverse relationships (outgoing, incoming, or both)

rel_types.description: Optional: relationship types to traverse. If omitted, all types are considered.

QueryOptions.description: Optional presentation hints that control response shape without affecting query semantics. Only 'dynamic_columns' and 'include_debug_sql' are recognized; all other fields are ignored.

dynamic_columns.description: Columns fetched for dynamically-discovered entities (PathFinding, Neighbors hydration). '*' returns all columns; 'default' returns the entity's default_columns from the ontology. Defaults to 'default'.

include_debug_sql.description: When true, includes compiled ClickHouse SQL in the response metadata for debugging. On SaaS: honored for GitLab team members. On self-managed/Dedicated: honored for instance admins. Defaults to false.

DynamicColumnMode.description: Controls column selection for dynamically-discovered entities during hydration.
